_meta.name: NEMESIS CVE pattern database
_meta.purpose: Structured root-cause patterns for directed generation, differential-oracle prioritization, and crash-to-CVE variant matching. Metadata + trigger SHAPES only — no exploit code.

schema.bug_class: type-confusion | uaf | oob | int-overflow | uninit | canonicalization | miscompile | sandbox-escape
schema.trigger_shape: structural description NEMESIS can bias generation toward
schema.nemesis_strategy: the strategy/generator that targets this shape (or null)

_meta.status: 486 real, source-linked entries. 61 curated (24 web-verified with full root-cause trigger shapes + 37 well-known browser-engine CVEs) carry a nemesis_strategy where one fits; the remaining ~425 were pulled from the live NVD 2.0 API by seeds/tools/scrape_cves.py (real CVE ids + descriptions, engine/bug_class assigned by a description-keyword heuristic — ~226 remain bug_class=unknown pending manual trigger-shape authoring). Every entry links to its nvd.nist.gov detail page. No fabricated entries.
_meta.verified: 2026-07-21 — curated set cross-checked against NVD; bulk set fetched live from NVD 2.0 (services.nvd.nist.gov)

cves.component: WasmGCTypedOptimizationReducer (Turboshaft)
cves.trigger_shape: wasm-gc value flowing through a SINGLE-BLOCK loop; Turboshaft type analysis narrows the ref type incorrectly because it mishandles single-block loop back-edges, then emits code assuming the narrowed (wrong) type.

cves.component: wasm type canonicalization (CanonicalEquality::EqualValueType)
cves.trigger_shape: two indexed reference types identical except for NULLABILITY; canonical equality ignores nullability so the engine treats (ref $t) and (ref null $t) as the same canonical type -> a nullable value passes a non-null cast/site.

cves.component: JS-to-Wasm call wrapper
cves.trigger_shape: JS calls an exported wasm function with argument types the optimized JS-to-wasm wrapper mis-assumes; a single bug yields both R/W and heap-sandbox bypass.

cves.component: V8 (OOB r/w)
cves.trigger_shape: crafted HTML/JS drives V8 to an out-of-bounds read+write inside the sandbox; 5th Chrome 0-day of 2026, fixed in 149.0.7827.103.

cves.component: V8 type confusion
cves.trigger_shape: type confusion in V8 reachable from a malicious page; discovered by Google TAG (Clement Lecigne), exploited in the wild, out-of-cycle fix 2025-11-17.

cves.component: V8 type confusion
cves.trigger_shape: sibling type-confusion to 13223 in the same Nov-2025 batch.

cves.component: Chrome (starts in renderer sandbox)
cves.trigger_shape: Chrome 0-day, active exploitation Feb 2026; renderer-sandbox reachable.

cves.component: Chrome
cves.trigger_shape: one of the five Chrome 2026 in-the-wild 0-days (metadata pending detailed root cause).

cves.component: Mojo/ipcz IPC (Windows handle)
cves.trigger_shape: incorrect Windows handle provided across the Mojo/ipcz boundary lets a compromised renderer escape the sandbox; Operation ForumTroll. NOTE: NEMESIS characterizes but does NOT build escape PoCs.

cves.component: JIT compiler
cves.trigger_shape: JIT miscompilation makes wrong assumptions about object lifetimes; optimized code holds a reference to memory freed by GC -> UAF, RCE with no interaction beyond page visit.

cves.component: JavaScript engine
cves.trigger_shape: engine uses memory after free; sibling to 2764/2766 in the automated-research batch.

cves.component: JIT compiler
cves.trigger_shape: JIT improperly handles memory during JS execution -> UAF.

cves.component: JIT
cves.trigger_shape: Firefox JIT RCE via miscompilation.

cves.component: JavaScript engine
cves.trigger_shape: use-after-free in the JS engine.

cves.component: DFG JIT
cves.trigger_shape: DFG emits code under a type assumption; attacker changes object shape so the assumption no longer holds -> type confusion -> R/W in WebContent. iOS 18.4-18.5, fixed 18.6.

cves.component: DFG Store Barrier Insertion Phase
cves.trigger_shape: a Phi node escapes but its Upsilon inputs are not marked escaped, so a later store misses a GC write barrier -> UAF. DarkSword iOS chain.

cves.component: ANGLE (GPU translation)
cves.trigger_shape: OOB in ANGLE used as the second link of the DarkSword iOS Safari chain (WebContent -> GPU).

cves.component: WebAssembly (WASM-GC)
cves.trigger_shape: WASM-GC type confusion; TyphoonPWN winning Chrome sandbox RCE. rec-group / cast handling.

cves.component: WASM-GC isorecursive type system
cves.trigger_shape: isorecursive type-system implementation flaw in the type validator; malformed rec-group subtyping accepted.

cves.component: V8 type confusion -> WASM RWX
cves.trigger_shape: integer/type confusion in TurboFan (range analysis of comparison of unsigned/signed); Magnitude EK. Historical, well-documented root cause.

cves.component: IonMonkey type inference
cves.trigger_shape: IonMonkey mishandles __proto__ mutations vs inferred types -> type confusion. Classic reference pattern.

cves.component: IonMonkey StoreElementHole / range
cves.trigger_shape: Ion range analysis flaw on array element stores; in-the-wild with a Firefox 0-day. Reference JIT bug.
